programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Jurors learned more about Yazeed Essa's extramarital affairs he had going on while he was married to his wife. On the stand, was his longtime mistress, who was also married to another man. She admitted she and the defendant were intimate for months leading to Rosemarie's death. Marguerita Montanez told jurors where they would usually meet up to engage in sexual relations. She claimed that she had no clue that Yazeed's defense team would implicate her in Rosemarie's death.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Jurors listened to more testimony from the defendant's longtime mistress. Marguerita Montanez happened to be the same mistress the Defense team alluded to jurors could be the real killer in Rosemarie Essa's poisoning death. Montanez found herself caught in the middle of Rosemarie's murder and Yazeed fleeing U.S. authorities as a suspect... She testified that Yazeed never gave her gifts, trips or even went on dates.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.sub-title: Sordid Love Triangle Leads To Murder
programme.desc: The second woman in Yazeed Essa's love triangle testified that her affair with him was just casual, not an infatuation. Marguerita Montanez told jurors that neither of them saw a future together. Their affair went on for months leading up to his wife Rosemarie's death. Next, cross-examination began on this longtime mistress. At the time, Rosemarie's death was ruled a homicide; local authorities treated both of the defendant's two main mistresses as possible suspects to the murder.

programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and several spurned lovers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, a maid discovered that a copy machine, placed in a busy Las Vegas casino, was actually a bomb.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This special episode examines previous cases to learn about the investigative techniques used by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1995, a South Carolina family was taken hostage as part of a bank robbery. Local police quickly identified two of the perpetrators but the criminal mastermind behind it all proved to be more elusive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A mother discovered her young son was missing from school. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. But when the few leads quickly turned cold, the FBI was called in to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 1999 and 2000, John Eric Armstrong cruised the streets of Detroit, Michigan looking for sex workers.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Baton Rouge Serial Killer, known for murdering seven women in the area and sentenced to death for his chilling crimes.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: New Jersey native Richard Kuklinski ran a burglary ring, distributed pirated pornography and dealt in weapons and drugs. He also claimed to have murdered over 100 people while working as a hit man for New York and New Jersey's crime families.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Michael Stephani has become etched in the minds of all who knew him as the notorious weepy voiced Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Colin Pitchfork is a child murder who killed fifteen-year-olds Lynda Mann and Dawn Ashworth in the 1980s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Being behind bars only made Italian-American mobster Freddy Geas even more brutal.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After murdering his aunt and uncle, Ramon Escobar rampages across L.A. and strangles his cellmate in prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Craig Bjork's series of murders are described as one of the worst cases of mass murder that the city of Minneapolis has ever seen. After killing two women and both of his children, he remains a dangerous threat - even behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of serial killer Geoffrey Evans and his accomplice John Shaw, who hit headlines in 1976 after the murders of two women in Ireland.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stuart Campbell was convicted of the murder of his 15-year-old niece in 2002, even though her body has never been found.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Years of unconnected home murders finally came together when cold-case detectives saw the pattern. They discovered that Emanuel, who moved far away, continued killing with the same familiar method.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a Pennsylvania man checks into a hospital for leg pain and later dies of thallium poisoning, police investigate his wife and find her in the process of collecting an insurance payment for her previous husband's death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Series premiere. A flight attendant went missing and was never seen again. Police proved her husband guilty of murder without finding the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother claims that an intruder murdered her two sons, but lacking a motive, police use blood-spatter analysis and behavioral profiling to reconstruct a different scenario.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a surgeon is found dead in his home, police assume it was suicide. The investigation reveals otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Timothy Wilson Spencer was identified as the man who raped and strangled five young women in Virginia. With the use of DNA profiling, an innocent man is freed and the real killer is convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For more than a year, hateful letters were sent to a school teacher in Pennsylvania. DNA analysis would eventually help seal the perpetrator's fate.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Who was the sexual offender who murdered two boys in Nebraska? The key to stopping him would be the unique composition of the "junk rope" he used to tie his victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1998, an evening out at a Maryland murder mystery theatre performance turns into a real-life whodunit when the badly burned body of Stephen Hricko is discovered in his hotel room after a fire.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Scott Dunn was missing and when the police sprayed his bedroom with Luminol, a scene of horrific violence emerged. Now investigators faced a daunting task.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-man shrimp boat sinks when a fast-moving, violent storm strikes the Louisiana coast. The captain drowns, but the deckhand is rescued fourteen hours later. The crewman is hailed as a hero - until the captain's body is discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping but didn't return home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1989, 4-year-old April Loveless was found dead in the backyard of her Texas home. Her mother Debbie Loveless and stepfather John Miller told police that she was attacked and killed by the family dogs. Investigators did not agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she has no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it's a hunting accident - until they find a strange letter, allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fire erupted in Kings Cross Station, killing 31 people and injuring dozens more. Investigators pinpointed the cause of the fire with state-of-the-art technology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a search for the killer of two teenagers in Texas, a behavioral profile led to suspect Jason Massey, and hard science proved the profile was correct. Massey was executed by lethal injection on April 3, 2001.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting leaves one man dead and another seriously wounded. Cell phone calls and shell casings point to a suspect, but authorities are unable to place him at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a residential community saw anything the day one of their neighbors was brutally murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A coroner finds telltale clues on the badly burned body of a murder victim, sending police in pursuit of a couple of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman's bone marrow test reveals arsenic poisoning, investigators try to determine if it is due to groundwater contamination or something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sometime during a neighborhood Christmas party, five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared. Investigators turned to forensic science to help them see what the witnesses missed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, Marie Robards suffered the loss of her father due to a heart attack - but it was more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carried manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, San Francisco pornographer Artie Mitchell was murdered in his home. His brother and business partner Jim is found near the scene carrying a rifle and later confesses to shooting Artie. The question is whether the shooting was premeditated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife Ruby Morris had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The results of the investigation surprised everyone...especially Earl Morris.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body. Finally, investigators use a single tooth found among some charred remains to identify her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A high school gym teacher gives his friend a ride home but never returns; his wife and friend receive letters saying he started a new life, but they suspect foul play despite no body being discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight takes the life of a national wrestling champion, police must determine if he was killed in cold blood or in self-defense.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A sting operation results in the recovery of over 12 ounces of meth. An alarm goes off at a coffee shop. A report of a stolen vehicle at a gas station.

programme.desc: A boater is reported to be under the influence of drugs or alcohol. Open containers and drugs are found in a suspicious vehicle. Deputies investigate individuals stealing recycling material.

programme.desc: An illegal marijuana grow operation is found in a mobile home. A deputy responds to a report of a loud and violent neighbor. A home burglary is interrupted by the homeowners.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies struggle with a wanted man who had too much to drink. A deputy deals with two young thieves. A young man bleeding from the head gives an officer a difficult time.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County

programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of domestic abuse along with an assault with a deadly weapon. A traffic stop on a vehicle with an expired registration reveals three women who have some surprising drug habits.

programme.desc: A suspicious person is carrying large swords in his backpack. A traffic stop results in one passenger taking off on foot. A search warrant is executed for a grow operation. The arrest of a gang member who has drugs.

programme.desc: A 9-1-1 call about a man threatening to kill his family. The investigation of a shoplifting case leads police to over 66 grams of black tar heroin. A domestic violence call.

programme.desc: Two children are found riding on the top of a vehicle. A probation search on a lady who has outstanding warrants.

programme.desc: Deputies wishing to be part of the SWAT team face a testing procedure that is harsh and demanding.
